required_header.download_date: ClinicalTrials.gov processed this data on March 2026
required_header.link_text: Link to the current ClinicalTrials.gov record.

clinical_study.brief_title: Docetaxel in Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer with BRCA2 Mutation and AR-V7 Positivity

brief_summary.textblock: This study evaluates docetaxel-based chemotherapy in patients with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) who have progressed on androgen receptor pathway inhibitors (ARPI), harbor somatic BRCA2 mutations, and have AR-V7 splice variant positivity on circulating tumor cell assay. Patients must have bone metastatic disease and ongoing androgen deprivation therapy. The primary endpoint is PSA response rate at 12 weeks.

detailed_description.textblock: Patients with mCRPC and documented progression on at least one prior ARPI (enzalutamide or abiraterone) are eligible. AR-V7 positivity on CTC assay is required, supporting the rationale for switching to taxane-based therapy. Somatic BRCA2 pathogenic mutation must be confirmed on tumor tissue or liquid biopsy NGS panel. Castrate testosterone levels (less than 50 ng/dL) must be maintained throughout the study. Patients receive docetaxel 75 mg/m2 IV every 3 weeks with prednisone 5 mg twice daily. Bone-targeting agents (denosumab or zoledronic acid) are allowed concurrently. Patients with mild to moderate chronic kidney disease (CKD stage 3) are eligible if GFR is adequate for dosing.

clinical_study.study_design: Intervention Model: Single Group Assignment, Primary Purpose: Treatment

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Histologically confirmed prostatic adenocarcinoma
- Metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC): PSA progression and/or radiographic progression despite castrate testosterone levels (less than 50 ng/dL)
- Prior treatment with at least one androgen receptor pathway inhibitor (enzalutamide or abiraterone) with documented disease progression
- AR-V7 splice variant positive by circulating tumor cell (CTC) assay
- Somatic BRCA2 pathogenic mutation confirmed by tumor NGS or liquid biopsy
- Bone metastases (axial and/or appendicular skeleton) on bone scan
- Ongoing androgen deprivation therapy (LHRH agonist or antagonist)
- Age 60 years or older
- ECOG performance status 0 to 2
- Adequate organ function: ANC greater than or equal to 1.5, platelets greater than or equal to 100, hemoglobin greater than or equal to 8.0 g/dL, creatinine less than or equal to 2.0x ULN (CKD stage 3 acceptable)
- Symptomatic bone pain permitted; adequate analgesia required for enrollment
Exclusion Criteria:
- Visceral metastases (lung, liver) as dominant site of disease
- AR-V7 negative on CTC assay
- BRCA1 mutation (without BRCA2 co-mutation)
- Prior taxane-based chemotherapy for prostate cancer
- Prior PARP inhibitor therapy
- Spinal cord compression or impending fracture requiring urgent intervention
- ECOG performance status 3 or 4
- Active symptomatic cardiac disease: New York Heart Association Class III or IV heart failure, uncontrolled arrhythmia
- Active hepatic metastases with AST/ALT greater than 3x ULN
- Neuroendocrine or small cell differentiation of prostate cancer
